entry.title: 2026 SAP BTP GTM strategy and priority initiatives for Partners
entry.content:
If you’re planning your 2026 GTM motions, this is one session you’ll want on your calendar.

SAP Business Technology Platform is no longer just “the platform behind the scenes”.  It’s becoming the engine of a consumption-led, AI-first go-to-market strategy — and Partners play a central role in making that real for customers.

In this executive-level webinar, SAP’s BTP Customer Success and Ecosystem leaders will walk through how SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, CIAM, and Core AI fit together as one coherent story.  Not in theory, but in actual GTM scenarios Partners can use to drive pipeline, bookings, and long-term consumption.

We’ll look at how SAP BTP shows up across RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP, why cACV matters more than ever, and how partners can align clean core principles with real customer outcomes.  Plus, we’ll share concrete partner-led GTM plays, and enablement programs designed to help you scale.

If you’re focused on sharpening your SAP BTP value proposition and building repeatable GTM motions for 2026, this session is for you.

👉 Register now  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012968.html

#SAP #SAPPartners #SAPBTP #PartnerGTM #SAPBuild #EnterpriseAI

entry.title: SAP Developer News, February 5th, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

SAP Build Q4 2025 Release Highlights

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/tooling-sap-build-blog-posts/what-s-new-in-sap-build-q4-2025-release-highlights/bc-p/14316927

My Home in SAP BTP ABAP Environment

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/my-home-in-sap-btp-abap-environment/ba-p/14314918

 

Did You Know? #2 – Mocking Auth in CAP

Devtoberfest 2025 – Winners Announcement

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest-blog-posts/devtoberfest-2025-winners-announcement/ba-p/14320779

New live stream series and blog post series on CAP topics

  • Modules, modularity & reuse in CDS models (blog post series) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2026/01/01/modules-modularity-and-reuse-in-cds-models/
  • Hands-on SAP Dev CXL live stream series playlist https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQCEU8XcyqnA5wYEZGxMPm6B
  • A new Hands-on SAP Dev mini-series on the core expression language in CDS (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2025/12/09/a-new-hands-on-sap-dev-mini-series-on-the-core-expression-language-in-cds/
  • Constraints, expressions and axioms in action (blog post) https://qmacro.org/blog/posts/2026/01/27/constraints-expressions-and-axioms-in-action/

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 SAP Build Q4 2025 Release Highlights

1:36 My Home in SAP BTP ABAP Environment

3:04 DYK #2 Mocking Auth in CAP

4:10 Devtoberfest 2025 – Winners Announcement

4:44 New live stream series and blog post series on CAP topics

Transcription

00;00;00;29 - 00;00;05;28
Announcer Guy
This is the SAP Developer News for February 5th, 2026.

00;00;06;00 - 00;00;09;25
Shrini Neelamegam
Q4 of 2025 was a huge season for SAP Build,

00;00;09;27 - 00;00;40;11
Shrini Neelamegam
and we have got some powerful new features to talk about. First up, the agent builder in Joule Studio is officially GA. With MCP server integration and the ability to link multiple agents together. You can now build production grade AI agents that actually moves the needle for your business. Plus, Joule is now baked into every edition of Work Zone, bringing context aware answers and proactive insights right to your team's fingertips.

00;00;40;13 - 00;01;07;17
Shrini Neelamegam
For the developers out there, you can now spin up Freestyle UI5 apps in SAP Build Code with a simple slash command. And if you are looking to automate, you have added new ways to trigger processes and build process automation, making it easier to bridge the gap between SAP and non SAP systems. On the design front end SAP mobile start just got a makeover with Apple's liquid glass look.

00;01;07;20 - 00;01;35;14
Shrini Neelamegam
And for the admins, Workzone sites can now handle thousands of documents and translations. We are just scratching the surface here. There is plenty more on decision tables, decision rules, HTML templates for notifications, and new CAPannotations. Head over to Frederick's latest blog post for full Q4 2025 release announcement.

00;01;35;17 - 00;02;07;13
Sheena M K
SAP has rolled out My Home, the new personalized, light entry page for the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. By default, from at least 2602, and it will be the one and only entry page from release 2608. My home is designed to boost productivity with a modular, customizable layout that includes key sections such as news pages, add and insights, and cards, all aimed at giving users quick access to the tools and information they interact with the most.

00;02;07;15 - 00;02;40;00
Sheena M K
While the new section keeps you updated with the latest company or system updates, beta section offers quick access to your most used or favorite pages. The apps section improves discoverability with favorite, recent and most used apps. Plus, AI powered recommendations based on role assignments via SAP Business AI. The inside 30s provide immediate visibility into KPIs and analytics and data, offering quick access to apps for further details.

00;02;40;03 - 00;03;06;08
Sheena M K
Following the “Make It Mine” approach, users can hide, reorder and restructure sections. The skew then working side across all devices. So start exploring my home for a clean, organized and smarter navigation for the SAP BTP ABAP Environment. And for details, check out the blog post from Laura in the description below.

00;03;06;10 - 00;03;33;19
DJ Adams
Did you know that CAP has a mocked authentication strategy you can use in local development? So you don't have to put off this important part of your domain design? Here's a simple service annotated with some auth requirements. Authentication is required generally, and there are some role based restrictions on one of the entities. With CDS watch we get this strategy out of the box, as we can see, along with some users and roles too.

00;03;33;21 - 00;04;01;05
DJ Adams
Here's Alice with the admin role. For example. Let's explore. How about trying to read the Book Stocks Entity? 401! We need to authenticate, of course. Let's authenticate as Alice. Now we have read access. What about write? 403. Nope. Alice doesn't have the requisite role. Let's fix that with some extra config in dot cdsrc dot Json, for example, which assigns her the back office role that's required.

00;04;01;08 - 00;04;09;21
DJ Adams
Okay, let's try again. Success output request as Alice is now accepted. Nice.

00;04;09;23 - 00;04;31;17
Kevin Kiedelsheimer
Hey, SAP Community, it's finally time! We have the full list of the ten Devtoberfest 2025 winners ready and online. You can find a link to the blog post down in the description of this video, so you can head over to the SAP community and see if you are one of the approved winners of last year's Devtoberfest.

00;04;31;20 - 00;04;42;27
Kevin Kiedelsheimer
And if you're one of the winners, you can look forward to a great fully paid trip to SAP TechEd this year. I can't wait to see you there!

00;04;43;00 - 00;05;21;13
DJ Adams
The developer advocates mission is to help developers. That's you be successful. To that end, we create lots of content in various forms CodeJam, tutorials, videos, talks, and lots more. We just wanted to highlight a couple of recent series in the CAP area. First, there's a blog post series on the topic of CDS modeling. The series is called Modules Modularity and Reuse in CDS models, and it explores how we can use modules for reuse in and across our projects and beyond.

00;05;21;15 - 00;05;53;15
DJ Adams
It illustrates this by first creating and publishing a simple passive reuse module before moving on to understand, explore and dig into the active Capire common reuse module. We're also running a series right now on our hands on SAP Devs live stream show. It's on CAP’s Expression Language, C XL, and we're very happy to have Patrice Bender from the Cap compiler team take us through the beautiful detail in this show and tell style series.

00;05;53;17 - 00;06;21;10
DJ Adams
As always, driven by curiosity and the desire to understand. By the way, there's also a related blog post just published that explains some of the amazing detail that Patrice revealed in showing how constraints are realized by pushing down to the database. This week sees the third livestream episode in this series. You can join live at the usual hands on SAP Dev time.

00;06;21;10 - 00;06;29;09
DJ Adams
It's Friday mornings in Europe, and of course you can always watch the replays at your leisure links in the description.

entry.title: Future proofing enterprise agility with SAP Integration Suite and clean core
entry.content:
Enterprise agility sounds great on paper.  In reality?  It often feels like a constant balancing act between innovation and stability.

The thing is, many organizations are still trying to move fast while carrying years of technical debt.  That’s where SAP’s clean core philosophy — powered by SAP Integration Suite — really starts to make a difference.

In this upcoming partner session, we’ll walk through how clean core integrations and modern integration patterns help enterprises stay flexible without compromising their core.  You’ll hear practical guidance, real-world examples, and lessons learned from customers who are actively modernizing their landscapes today.

If you work with customers navigating complex integrations, hybrid environments, or long-term transformation roadmaps, this session is designed with you in mind.  Honestly, it’s about building agility that lasts — not quick fixes that create tomorrow’s problems.

Join us to explore how a future-proof integration strategy can unlock real business value, while keeping the core clean.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012943.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPIntegrationSuite #CleanCore #EnterpriseIntegration

entry.title: New course: Positioning SAP Order Management Services
entry.content:
We’re excited to introduce a brand-new learning opportunity for sales and pre-sales professionals:

Positioning SAP Order Management Services 

Why SAP Order Management Services?

In today’s digital-first economy, businesses face increasing challenges in managing their order processes. From handling multiple sales channels and meeting rising customer expectations to integrating with legacy systems and reducing errors, order management has never been more complex. SAP Order Management Services (OMS) is engineered to solve exactly these challenges.

What Will You Learn?

This focused course goes beyond product features. It guides you through:

  • The challenges faced by modern businesses in order management: Understand the pain points your customers are experiencing, such as supply chain visibility gaps, inefficiencies in order handling, and the need for real-time inventory management.
  • The end-to-end capabilities of SAP OMS: Discover how SAP OMS covers the full order process lifecycle, from order capture and validation to fulfillment, tracking, and returns, seamlessly integrated across all channels and touchpoints.
  • Business value drivers and tangible benefits: Learn how to articulate the business case, showing customers how SAP OMS improves operational efficiency, elevates the customer experience, reduces costs, boosts revenue, and supports scalability and innovation.
  • Inspiring customer success stories: See how real-world organizations have transformed their order management processes and driven measurable business outcomes with SAP OMS, stories you can share to build trust and credibility with your clients.

Who Should Enroll?

  • Sales consultants working with SAP customers or prospects
  • Pre-sales consultants looking to stand out with specialized knowledge
  • Anyone eager to understand how SAP OMS drives smarter business operations

Learn Today!

Ready to master SAP OMS positioning? Start learning now and take the first step toward equipping yourself with the knowledge and strategies you need to confidently position SAP Order Management Services.

 

We hope you enjoy this course and encourage you to share your valuable feedback with us. Thank you!

entry.title: Joule Is Not a Chatbot: How AI Agents on SAP BTP Help North American Utilities Reduce Risk
entry.content:
Introduction

 In North American utilities, regulatory expectations shape almost every operational decision. Over the years working with U.S. and Canadian utilities, I’ve seen how state and provincial rules influence even the smallest processes — from how quickly meter reads must be validated to how consistently billing exceptions must be resolved.

Regulators typically define:

  • Required timelines for completing meter reads

  • SLA windows for resolving billing exceptions

  • Limits on consecutive estimated bills

  • Documentation standards for every action taken

When these expectations aren’t met, the consequences are real: customer credits, revenue adjustments, additional reporting, and increased regulatory scrutiny. Even with strong CIS and MDM systems, I’ve seen exception queues grow faster than teams can manage. SLA timers often run in the background without real-time visibility, and escalations still depend heavily on someone noticing an issue manually.

This gap between regulatory expectations and operational reality is exactly where AI agents can make a meaningful difference.

Main Content

1. Regulatory Context for North American Utilities

In North American utilities, regulatory expectations shape almost every operational decision. Over the years working with U.S. and Canadian utilities, I’ve seen how state and provincial rules influence even the smallest processes — from how quickly meter reads must be validated to how consistently billing exceptions must be resolved.

Regulators typically define required timelines, SLA windows, limits on estimated bills, and documentation standards. When these expectations aren’t met, utilities face customer credits, revenue adjustments, additional reporting, and increased scrutiny. Exception queues grow quickly, SLA timers run without visibility, and escalations depend on manual intervention.

This is where AI agents can make a meaningful difference.

2. Why Joule Is More Than a Chatbot

During my learning journey with Joule, one misconception stood out immediately: many people assume Joule is “just another chatbot.” But when you look at how it works on SAP BTP, it becomes clear that Joule is fundamentally different.

A chatbot answers questions. A Joule Agent takes action.

From a practitioner’s perspective, what makes Joule valuable is its ability to:

  • Understand business rules and regulatory requirements

  • Monitor operational data continuously

  • Detect exceptions before they breach SLAs

  • Trigger automated workflows

  • Maintain complete audit logs for compliance

In regulated industries like utilities, this shift from conversational assistance to policy-driven automation is significant. It’s not about chatting — it’s about operational discipline.

 

3. Why AI Agents Matter for Exception Management

Exception management has always been one of the most resource-intensive areas in utilities. Across multiple programs, I’ve seen the same patterns repeat:

  • No-read or zero-consumption meters

  • High/low usage variances

  • Billing holds

  • Field-service-dependent issues

  • High-bill complaints

Each of these carries its own SLA clock. When thousands of exceptions accumulate, manual monitoring simply can’t keep up — even with experienced teams.

AI agents help by addressing the exact pain points I’ve seen utilities struggle with:

3.1 Continuous Monitoring

Agents watch exceptions as they occur, not hours or days later.

3.2 Risk-Based Prioritization

They highlight issues that could cause regulatory or revenue impact — something analysts often have to do manually.

3.3 Autonomous Escalation

They trigger workflows or notify supervisors without waiting for human review, reducing the dependency on tribal knowledge.

 

4. How Joule Agents Work on SAP BTP

A Joule Agent is not a standalone tool — it’s an orchestrator that brings multiple BTP services together.

4.1 Data and Event Integration

It listens to events from systems such as:

  • SAP S/4HANA Utilities

  • SAP MDM

  • SAP Event Mesh

  • SAP HANA Cloud

In real operations, these events include new exceptions, updated SLA timers, meter reads, or billing document changes.

4.2 Policy and Rule Interpretation

Regulatory rules are translated into thresholds, categories, and escalation paths — something utilities typically manage through spreadsheets or manual SOPs.

4.3 Workflow Execution

The agent can start SAP Build Process Automation workflows, reassign cases, or notify supervisors.

4.4 Audit Logging

Every action is logged for compliance — a major benefit for regulatory reporting.

 

5. Practical Example: SLA Monitoring for Meter-Reading Exceptions

Here’s a scenario I’ve seen repeatedly: A utility must resolve meter-reading exceptions within 48 hours. These include no-reads, communication failures, and high/low variances.

Without AI Agents

  • Exceptions pile up

  • Analysts manually review queues

  • Supervisors rely on periodic reports

  • SLA timers aren’t monitored continuously

  • Escalations depend on human judgment

This often leads to missed deadlines and inconsistent documentation — especially during peak seasons.

 

Figure 1: SLA monitoring lifecycle for meter-reading exceptions using Joule Agents.

 

With a Joule Agent

Step 1: Monitors exceptions and SLA timers in real time Step 2: Flags high-risk or time-sensitive cases Step 3: Automatically triggers workflows, reassigns cases, and notifies supervisors Step 4: Captures all actions for compliance

Outcome:

  • Fewer SLA breaches

  • Less revenue leakage

  • Stronger compliance

  • Better use of staff time

From a practitioner’s perspective, this is exactly the kind of automation utilities have been trying to achieve for years.

 

6. Reference Architecture

A typical setup includes:

  • SAP S/4HANA Utilities

  • SAP MDM

  • SAP Event Mesh

  • SAP HANA Cloud

  • Joule Agent

  • SAP Build Process Automation

  • SAP Audit Log Service

This architecture can be rolled out gradually and adapted to existing landscapes — something important for utilities with complex legacy environments.

 
Figure 2: Reference architecture for Joule Agent orchestration across SAP BTP services.

 

7. Governance and Compliance

Regulators expect utilities to demonstrate:

  • Proactive monitoring

  • Consistent rule application

  • Clear escalation paths

  • Strong audit evidence

Joule Agents help by applying rules consistently, reducing manual errors, and maintaining detailed logs — all of which strengthen compliance posture.

 

8. Organizational Impact

Utilities that adopt AI agents typically see improvements in:

  • Exception resolution speed

  • SLA compliance

  • Revenue protection

  • Operational efficiency

  • Employee workload balance

Supervisors gain real-time visibility, analysts focus on solving issues instead of chasing timers, and compliance teams get better documentation.

 

Conclusion

Joule is not a chatbot. It’s an AI agent framework designed to help utilities automate compliance, reduce revenue leakage, and manage exceptions proactively. Based on what I’ve seen across multiple utility programs, the shift from reactive exception handling to automated, risk-aware operations is becoming essential — and Joule Agents on SAP BTP provide a practical path to get there.

entry.title: Transform Your Customer Engagement with SAP Customer Data Cloud – Join Our Live Session!

entry.title: Unlock Seamless Identity Management with Our SAP Customer Data Cloud REST API Live Session!
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital world, efficient identity management is the cornerstone of secure and streamlined customer interactions. Are you ready to revolutionize the way you handle customer identities? We invite you to join our compelling live session Seamless Customer Identity Management: Mastering SAP Customer Data Cloud REST APIs on March 12, 2026, 15:00 - 16:30 SGT or 9:00 - 10:30 CET.

Why Attend?

This interactive session is designed to empower you with the knowledge and skills necessary to master SAP Customer Data Cloud REST API. Whether you are a technical consultant, functional consultant, or administrator, this session will provide valuable insights that cater to your specific needs.

What’s on the Agenda?

Prepare to dive deep into the world of REST API as our team of experts guide you through a transformative learning experience. Here's what you'll achieve by the end of the session:

  • A Comprehensive Understanding of REST APIs: Gain a robust understanding of the full range of SAP Customer Data Cloud REST APIs and how they can streamline your identity management processes.
  • Master Key API Tools and Methods: Learn how to leverage essential API tools and methods for an efficient implementation that supercharges your identity flows.
  • Achieve Seamless and Secure Identity Flows: Implement the knowledge gained to deliver secure and effortless identity flows, enhancing customer experience and boosting operational efficiency.

Who Should Attend?

This session is tailored for a diverse audience, including:

  • Technical Consultants: Enhance your technical toolkit with advanced API strategies.
  • Functional Consultants: Gain insights into how APIs can optimize functional workflows.
  • Administrators: Acquire the skills to robustly manage customer identities with precision.

Join Us for an Engaging Experience!

Get ready to embark on a journey of transformation. This session promises to be engaging, interactive, and immensely beneficial for you and your team.

📅March 12, 2026

⏰16:00 - 17:30 SGT (9:00 - 10:30 CET)

💻Location: SAP Learning Site

Secure your spot today and take a decisive step towards mastering customer identity management with SAP Customer Data Cloud REST API. Register now and don’t miss out on this opportunity to enhance your technical prowess!

Please Note

For a better understanding of the topics presented in the live session, it’s recommended to check out the following:

  • From Onboarding to Governance: Mastering SAP Customer Data Cloud for B2B

We're excited to connect and explore the endless possibilities of SAP Customer Data Cloud REST API with you. See you there!

entry.title: Kick Off 2026 With Impact: Collaborate on 56 Fresh SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 56 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until March 13, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Ariba

  • Reporting and Analytics in SAP Ariba Invoicing

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-powered Code Upgrade Automation
  • BPTX Early Insights
  • Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users - provide feedback on new and planned features
  • SAP BTP Developer's Guide
  • SAP BTP: Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • Validate MCP Hub

SAP Concur

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
  • New User Onboarding Experience in SAP Concur
  • Reimagining Travel & Expense with SAP Concur-embedded virtual cards experience
  • SAP Concur - User Profile Management

  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Recommendations
  • Trip Extras - Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most.
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

Data & Analytics

  • Exploring BDC Web & Mobile Experiences to drive user relevancy and adoption
  • Product Insights / Product 360
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • Agentic AI - Analytical Business Insights  
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • AI-assisted task automation
  • AI in Core Logistics (HUM, SN, BM, MM-IM, Kanban)
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Customer returns user experience
  • E-mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Improving the Usability of Task and Application Logs in Group Reporting
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite
  • Understanding Payment Reconciliation Processes and Automation Barriers in Bank Statements
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

SAP Globalization

  • AI Agents for SAP Translation Hub

Others

  • Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot
  • How Designers and Developers Use SAP Design System Resources
  • Integrated Planning in Dairy
  • Research into an Intelligent Spare Parts Request Processing Agent
  • SAP Reference Architecture

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Partner learning accelerator: Certified academy for data analyst - SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Certification journeys can feel overwhelming.  Content everywhere, limited time, real project pressure.

That’s exactly why we created the Partner learning accelerator: Certified academy for data analyst – SAP Analytics Cloud.

This 6-hour live academy is designed for partners who want to prepare for the SAP Certified – Data Analyst – SAP Analytics Cloud certification with confidence.  Not just theory, but reinforced core concepts, hands-on practice, and exam-relevant topics you’ll actually use in customer projects.

We focus on data modeling, analysis, visualization, and planning—plus how these capabilities come together in real project scenarios.  The thing is, certification readiness isn’t just about passing an exam.  It’s about feeling ready when a customer asks the hard questions.

If you already understand analytics basics and have some exposure to SAP Analytics Cloud, this academy will help you connect the dots and sharpen your skills.

👉 Register now and invest six focused hours in your growth as an SAP partner.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2025/apr/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00009814.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPPartners #SAPAnalyticsCloud #SAPLearning #DataAnalytics

entry.title: Integrate planning and risk management with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Planning without risk is just optimism with formatting.

That’s why we’re bringing you “Integrate planning and risk management with SAP Analytics Cloud”.  A live session built for partners who want sharper decisions — fast.

With SAP Analytics Cloud Compass, planning stops being a one-number forecast and becomes a range of real, probability-based outcomes.  You’ll see how Monte Carlo simulations expose uncertainty, reduce manual scenario work, and give teams a shared language for decision-making.

No heavy math.
No black boxes.
Just intuitive simulations that business users can actually trust.

We’ll cover what risk-enhanced planning really means, show Compass in action, and explain why planning and risk were always meant to work together (long-lost twins, reunited).

If you’re focused on xP&A and smarter planning conversations with customers, don’t miss this.

👉 Register now and join us live.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/feb/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012965.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPAnalyticsCloud #SAPPartners #xPA #PlanningInnovation

entry.title: Discover the power of SAP BTP and SAP BDC partner academies
entry.content:
Partners don’t win with slides.

They win with confidence, clarity, and hands-on experience.

That’s what this webinar delivers.

In Discover the power of SAP Business Technology Platform and SAP Business Data Cloud partner academies, we’ll show how pre-configured SAP BTP and SAP Business Data Cloud landscapes help you run workshops, guided tours, and real-world exercises—without heavy setup or guesswork.

The thing is, when teams can touch the technology, everything changes.  Sales conversations get sharper.  Customer engagement feels more natural.  And solution expertise grows faster.  Plus, you’ll see how this SAP-led approach supports pipeline creation and maximizes the impact of RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP journeys.

If you’re looking for practical enablement that moves the needle—not just checks a box—this session is worth your time.

⚡ Register now https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jan/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012839.html

entry.title: The 2025 Developer Insights Survey: The Report
entry.content:
Role Overlaps for external SAP developers

Part I: Introduction and General Information​

Welcome to the 2025 SAP Developer Insights Survey report. This annual survey targets SAP’s existing external developer audience. It is used to track demographics, usage patterns, and trends within that population. 2025 is the fifth year we have conducted the survey. The survey program is led by the SAP Ecosystem and Community team. Key support is provided by the SAP Global Experience Management Office (XMO). Several other SAP teams provide advice and inputs to survey content. Those teams are listed at the end of this report.

The 2025 survey was open for six weeks - from January 22nd through March 7th.

Using social media announcements, SAP's Developer News show, and a web-popup on Community web sites, we invited all technical professionals in the SAP ecosystem to participate in the survey.

Each year's survey is composed of a mix of new topic questions and repeat select questions from past years to track trends.

Key Findings from the 2025 Survey​

  • Generative AI is Positively Affecting our Developer Ecosystem - While these tools are not exclusively Joule-based, roughly half of our respondents report evaluating Gen AI developer tooling and, among those using it, over half report that these tools have a "profound positive impact" in their real world use.

  • Low Code Use is Increasing - SAP Build low code tools lead in this space. Overall use of Low Code products on production projects continues to increase.

  • BTP Guidance Framework - limited awareness -- this important guidance document was announced at TechEd this past November, but - as with BTP Developers Guide in 2024 - barely half of respondents were aware of this document's existence.

  • Work-from-Home Continues Trending Down - we have been tracking these numbers since 2021. 6% fewer folks used their home as their primary workplace when compared to last year.

These topics will be covered in more detail in the rest of the report.

General Topics​

We included a number of general questions in the survey covering such topics as age, work location and status, as well as role.

Employment Status and Relation to SAP​

Employment

"What is your current employment status?"  
SAP Developer Community Composition
We have seen no significant change in these breakdowns from year to year.

SAP Developer Community Age Distribution

This shows increases in both the oldest age buckets for 2025. This was accompanied by similar decreases in the middle two age buckets ( ranging from 25 - 44 years of age). This merits deeper research as it potentially indicates either a gap in hiring younger employees to cover the normal aging of our community, or an outright loss of qualified younger staff. The two year trend in the 25-34 group reinforces that idea.

Where are the Developers?​

We asked, "Which country are you based in?"

By country, India has the largest number of respondents and probably the densest geographic concentration, followed by Germany, and then the USA. When grouped by SAP region, however, EMEA North is largest.

The proportions remain essentially unchanged from 2024.

Part II. Narrowing the Response Set

A primary objective of our survey is to build a model of our community's usage patterns. It helps us build more effective programs. With that goal in mind, in the remainder of the report we will narrow the data we'll consider in these ways:

  • External Developers Only - we exclude responses from SAP employees, and others (students, analysts, etc.)
  • Recent Development Experience - we only include responses from those who stated that they had been part of an SAP extension or integration project within the past year

When we filter the data set in this way, we are left with a set of 628 responses that we'll work with for the remainder of the report.

Workplace and Trends

Office Location
"As of right now, where do you primarily physically work from?"  
Year-to-year trend in Workplace
This shows a slowing trend of employees moving back to conventional offices from home. From a past Developer Survey, 29% of respondents reported they had primarily worked at home prior to the pandemic.

Recent SAP Project Experience?​

"Have you developed integrations or extensions for any SAP products or technologies in the past 12 months?" 

Extension and Integration are key scenarios for developers working with SAP applications. We wanted to focus on developers with recent experience in these areas. Roughly 70% of developers answered "Yes" here.

Popular Programming Languages​

"Which programming languages have you used in your development work over the past 12 months? Please select all that apply." 

This was posed as a multiple response question. ABAP dominates, as it has in every year of our survey. JavaScript is very popular as well, which could be attributed to its use in both SAPUI5 and CAP. Java and Python are roughly tied for third place. The relative positions of these top four languages hasn't changed much, year to year.

We extended our survey beyond just language use to ask about popular frameworks.

Popular Programming Frameworks​

We asked respondents who are Developers to pick which common front and back-end frameworks they have worked with in the past year.

Front-Ends

Back-Ends

Application and Business Technology Platform Architectures​

SAP applications can be extended using any one of several architectural approaches. These can be grouped into two major classes: on-stack (in-app) and side-by-side (essentially, BTP-based).

We were wondering which approaches are most popular.

Popular Extension Architectures

"For the most recent extension project that you were part of, what was the principal runtime architecture of the back-end elements?"  

We don't distinguish between newer and older SAP applications (e.g., S/4HANA vs. ECC) -- this likely explains the dominance of "on-stack ABAP". Aside from the obvious dominance of ABAP, we can also see that CAP is relatively popular. As with last year's results on this question, we see Low Code products like Build Apps and Build Process Automation are popular -- this question is asking about real production projects, and seeing those two account for roughly 10% of all extension projects highlights the value of these tools.

ABAP and CAP both have style or language variants in their respective frameworks. ABAP can be programmed as "ABAP for Cloud Development" or "ABAP Standard". CAP is available in JavaScript or Java language variants. Where the respondent selected ABAP or CAP, we asked about the variant used on the project:

 

ABAP Variants

CAP Variants

The rough 50-50 breakdown of ABAP styles is consistent with responses elsewhere in the survey. It reflects that roughly half of the ABAP projects are ECC or very early forms of S/4HANA. Importantly, the group that didn't understand the difference is considerably smaller than last year (2% versus 5% in 2024).

CAP/JavaScript is plainly most popular for CAP projects. Unlike last year, we do have respondents reporting the use of CAP/Java projects, although the percentage is modest - 5%.

Popular IDEs​

We are always curious about general work patterns of developers. We asked about developer's most commonly used editors. While it would be difficult in such a survey of all developers to add many questions, we were particularly curious about most popular IDE or editor.

 
Preferred IDE or Editor
"Which IDE or text editor do you most commonly use for development tasks?"  
Consistent with past year's results, the editor of choice will depend on the language. SAP GUI and -- arguably -- Eclipse are the primary editors for ABAP, so it's maybe no surprise that they are reflected as most popular. This is the first year that SAP Build Code appeared in the survey. We maintained separate choices for BAS and Build Code to see where folks might say that they have been using BAS, but have not yet transitioned to the broader capabilities offered by SAP Build Code.

Potential / Use of Generative AI for Developers​

Beginning with the 2024 survey, we asked developers about their thoughts and use of several major capabilities of Generative AI as it applies to software development.

Generative AI for Developers

"Which of these GenAI capabilities has your team explored or used? Select the choice that best describes your use of each capability. "  

An brief explanation of each category was included in the survey and is shown below:

Code Generation and Autocompletion: GenAI tools can generate code snippets, complete lines of code, or suggest entire functions based on the context provided by the developer.

Bug Detection and Code Review: AI models can be trained to identify potential bugs and vulnerabilities in the code. They can also suggest improvements in code quality, best practices, and code consistency.

Automated Testing and Test Data Generation: GenAI can be used to create test cases, which can help in thoroughly testing software applications. This includes generating test data that covers edge cases, potentially reducing the time and effort in manual testing.

Documentation Generation: AI can assist in generating and updating technical documentation. It can interpret the code and create comprehensive documentation, which is crucial for maintaining and scaling software projects.

Natural Language Processing (NLP) for Code: AI models can translate natural language queries into code, allowing developers to express their intent in plain language. This can be particularly helpful for novice developers working with unfamiliar frameworks.

Customized User Experience Design: AI can help generate user interface designs to enhance user experience.

AI-Powered Development Tools: Integration of AI in IDEs for real-time assistance, such as suggesting optimizations, refactoring code, or even predicting the next steps in development.

Automated Code Refactoring: AI can assist in code refactoring by suggesting or even implementing improvements in code structure.

Software Design and Architecture: AI can help in generating software design patterns or architectural models based on specified requirements. This can speed up the initial stages of development and ensure adherence to best practices.

Enhanced Security Protocols: Generative AI can be used to develop advanced security protocols and encryption methods, providing robust security solutions in software applications.

We also believed that Generative AI has been on the scene long enough for folks to being to experiment with putting it to use in meaningful ways. So, we included a couple of questions to gauge their perceptions of its utility to to their teams.

Current Gen AI Use

"How would you characterize your team’s current use of Generative AI tools or LLMs in your day-to-day work? Consider tools from any vendor or open source. Examples might include Joule, SAP Generative AI Hub, GitHub Copilot, or open source LLMs. (select the most applicable statement)"  

Gen AI Effectiveness for Developers

"Looking back on the past year, what is your own sense of how the use of Generative AI based tools or LLMs impacted the effectiveness or efficiency of your team?" 

Joule in SAP Build Code

Several Joule features were introduced into SAP Build Code prior to the survey. We asked respondents about their awareness or use of these features.

Joule for CDS Modeling

Joule for Test Data Set Generation

Joule Code Generation

Development Job Roles

We asked respondents which common development-related roles they perform.

"Which roles describe your day-to-day work? (Select all that apply)"

Respondents often "wear multiple hats" in their job. This question is asked in "select all that apply" form to capture that information.
The Venn diagram on the right depicts the relative overlaps of the top seven roles reported by respondents. The area of each overlapping region reflects the count respondents performing the overlapping roles. For example, from this we can seel that all (UX) "Designers" are also "Developers". Most but not quite all "Enterprise Architects" also are "Solution Architects".

These patterns of overlap are very similar to those in past years.

It is unsurprising that the "Developer" role leads. Architecture roles as a group have increased each year.

Development Domains​

For respondents selecting "Developer" as one of their roles, we were interested in the specific application Domains they work in:

"Which of the following development domains describe your day-to-day work? (select all that apply)"  
Year to year, Integration came in higher than we expected. Mobile web front-end development is more common than native mobile application development. This isn't completely unexpected, but worth noting.

 

Developer Satisfaction with SAP​

SAP Development Satisfaction
"For your recent experience developing an integration or extension, how would you describe your overall satisfaction with SAP?"  

Roughly 74% of respondents report they are “very satisfied” or “satisfied” with their development work with SAP. This is back near the 75% reported two years ago and also up from the 2024 figure, 70%.

We also gave all respondents a list of potential obstacles in their use of SAP tools and technologies and asked them to select the most significant pain points.

 
Obstacles in SAP tool us
"Which of these potential pain points are obstacles to your development when using SAP tools or frameworks? (select up to three most significant items)"  

BTP Guidance Framework

Awareness and Use of the BTP Guidance Framework
"The SAP BTP Guidance Framework was announced at SAP’s TechEd this past November. Which statement best described your level of familiarity with this document?"  
Nearly half of respondents were unaware of this document's release. Raising awareness for this and other related BTP framework documents would be a good goal for 2025.

Cloud Insights​

We first asked respondents if they used any cloud providers for their projects. For those that answered, "yes", we asked about the use of several major providers, including SAP BTP. We also asked if their use included SAP or non-SAP projects. This information was condensed into a chart depicting the relative use of each cloud provider.

Similar to the earlier questions around BTP Environments, these numbers do not reflect a tally of projects for each platform - instead it reflects developer exposure to each.

Using Cloud Providers?

"Are you using any cloud providers for your development projects?"  

We asked, "Which cloud providers are you using for development projects?" and we asked in a way to separate SAP-related projects from more general use.
 
SAP Business Technology Platform
Microsoft Azure

Amazon Web Services

 

Google Cloud Platform

Alibaba Cloud

Low / No Code Tools​

This is the third consecutive year in which we've asked questions around Low Code and No Code (LCNC) products. The list was composed of leaders in the then current Gartner LCNC Magic Quadrant Report and select SAP products. SAP Process Automation -- now Build Process Automation -- was not yet announced at the time of the 2022 survey, so it only appears in the more recent data.

For each product, we asked respondents to select the most applicable category reflecting their awareness or use. These questions were organized as a single large matrix of boxes in Qualtrics - There is no "I am unfamiliar with this product" response. Instead, the respondent simply would skip checking one of the other boxes.

It is important to point out that we are depicting response counts in the following charts rather than percentages and that the difference in total annual response counts must be considered to compare between years. The raw respondent count for 2023 was 2.4 times larger than 2022. And 2024 responses were less than half of 2023. We'll normalize for that in a later table.

These depictions can be misleading: our number of respondents each year varies. But we can normalize for that annual variation; what does this look like then? If we extract the data for production use for all products, we can use that to calculate the proportion use of each product by year (in other words, the sum of each column below totals one hundred percent). We can then visualize the relative shift in popularity over time:

 

Productive use of Product2022202320242025
Vendor A20%48%15%27%
SAP Build family (Build Apps + BPA)2%32%23%35%
Vendor B14%28%7%18%
SAP Signavio5%14%5%9%
Vendor C7%12%3%3%
Vendor D2%2%1%3%
Vendor E3%4%1%2%
Vendor F3%4%1%2%

SAP Build low code products dominate in our SAP ecosystem.

Learning and Help Resources​

We were interested in preferences in the format or media type of learning resources by respondents.

Preferred Learning Resources
"When learning new skills or technical subjects over the past 12 months, what resources did you tend use the most? Please select up to three resources."  

These results seem relatively unchanged year-to-year. One point of interest is the large difference in popularity between tutorials and MOOCs (53% versus 16% here). Tutorials tend to be hands-on. They are also broken into substantially smaller time chunks. This reinforces our hypothesis that different content types and required time investment have an impact in some learning scenarios. This merits investigation.

On the topic of Help, we shifted in this next question from formats to specific web sites.

 
Top SAP Help Resource Sites
"What is your preferred resource if you need help, or have a challenge with the SAP technology / tool you are working with? "  
Almost sixty percent of respondents say they use organic search or SAP Community as their primary source of SAP help. This is consistent with past years. This continues to demonstrate good visibility of the Community site overall and likely speaks well for the usefulness of the site content.

Satisfaction with SAP Learning Hub

SAP Learning Hub Awareness

"Which of the following best describes your exposure to the SAP Learning site?"

Overall Value of Learning Hub
"Overall how valuable are the resources on that site in helping you to understand SAP developer technologies?"  
Technical Certifications
"How many technology-related certification programs have you completed within the past four years?" 

Survey Methodology

This report is based on a Qualtrics web-based survey of 1,492 respondents from 58 countries. The survey ran for six calendar weeks between January 22 to March 7th, 2025. Ninety percent of the respondents invested ten minutes or less with the survey.

The survey was promoted via the SAP Community website and the SAP Developer Center, sap.com pop-up intercepts, social media posts.

Other References​

The annual Stack Overflow Developer Survey is an excellent (and no-cost) reference for industry-wide habits of developers.

entry.title: Creating a ‘Hello World’ Joule Agent
entry.content:
Building on Michael Ameling's excellent Agent Builder in Joule Studio announcement and The Joule Studio Learning Series, this post will guide you through building your first Joule Agent.

I'll walk you through the steps to build a "Hello, World" agent. Along the way, you'll get familiar with the basic workflow in SAP Build, explore the UI, avoid at least one common pitfall, and have your agent up and running in just a few minutes. By building the simplest possible agent, you'll gain the confidence that building agents is easy, and creating more sophisticated ones will feel like a natural next step. Let's get started.

Setup and Prerequisites
The setup of SAP Build Apps, the activation of Joule, and the SAP Agent Builder are described in these two blog posts:
  • How to Get Started with Joule Studio
  • Activate Joule Studio in 5 Simple Steps

I'll assume that's done, and we'll start our journey in the lobby of SAP Build Apps.

Creating a Project
Click on the "Create" button, and select "Joule Skill":
 
 
Enter a name and a description:
 

Creating the Joule Agent

You'll be forwarded to the overview of your project where you can create the Joule Agent:

 

Again, enter a name and a description. A proposal for the identifier will be generated automatically:

 
On the details page of your Hello World Agent, you could fill in the fields manually, but let's use Joule to create a proposal. In the top left, click on the Joule icon to open the Joule sidebar. Enter a prompt like this:

"Can you please update our existing Hello World agent by filling in the expertise, instructions and additional context? The agent should respond to every prompt with "Hello, World!"."

 
 
As a result, Joule updates the agent, and we're almost done. We just need to add a placeholder tool, for example the calculator, because Joule Studio does not allow an agent to be saved without a tool assignment:

 
Now you can save the agent, and we can move on to testing the functionality we've just implemented.

 

Testing the Joule Agent

When you click on the "Test" button (just above the "Save" button), you'll be asked which environment you'd like to test in. Select the environment associated with your email address.

If your email address doesn't appear in the dropdown, you need to activate your private environment first. To do so, click on the SAP Build icon in the top left corner, click on "Control Tower" in the left-hand menu, and then click on the "Environments" tile:

On the Environments screen, click the button to activate your private environment:

Afterwards, you can resume testing and your private environment should appear in the dropdown as described above.

Now we're ready to test the agent, for example with a prompt like "Let's test the agent".

 
 
As we can see, the agent correctly responds with "Hello, World!" 😅

To end the test session, just click on the "Stop Testing" button.

Deploying the Joule Agent

To get our agent out of the testing environment, we need to deploy it to a real environment.

Let's create a Hello World environment. Navigate to the Control Tower, select the Environments tile, and click on "Create". Enter a name and description for the environment. The proposal for the identifier will be generated automatically.

 

Your user will be automatically added to this new environment. If you see a blank screen when you want to view the environment details, just try refreshing the page in the browser. It may take a little while to generate.

Once the environment is active, we can deploy our agent. Click on the "Release" button in the agent overview.

 

In the subsequent pop-up, enter a release note and click on "Release".

 

Once released, switch to version 1.0.0 at the top of the screen by clicking on the dropdown next to the title.

 
 

In the released version, there is a "Deploy" button instead of the "Test" and "Release" buttons. Click on the "Deploy" button, select the Hello World environment, and click on "Deploy".

 
 
After a few moments, the status of agent version 1.0.0 will change to "Deployed and Active".

Testing the Deployment

As a final step in this tutorial, let's test our deployed agent in the Hello World environment.

Open the Hello World environment via the Control Tower and view the environment details by clicking on it in the list. There, you will see the deployment of the Hello World agent we just made.

To test it, open the Joule tab for this environment. Depending on your screen resolution, this may be hidden in the "More" drop-down. On this tab, click the "Launch" button.

 

Joule will open in a new tab and we can prompt it to test the agent, for example like this: "Run the Hello World agent."

To confirm that Joule actually called the agent, you can investigate the log by clicking on the icon in the top right of the title bar.

 
 

Conclusion

Congratulations, you just implemented your first Joule agent! As intended, the overall result is "just" a Hello World agent, but this is an important first step in your journey to becoming a Joule agent builder. You familiarized yourself with the UI and performed the full workflow from start to finish, including creating a project, creating an agent, testing, and deploying it. Along the way, we also used Joule itself to fill in the agent's configuration details.

From here, there are many more topics worth exploring, like Joule skills, MCP integration, or agent-to-agent communication. However, for me, it's always important to build a Hello World example first without getting lost in the details. With more complex starter scenarios, it's easy to get stuck on technical details and lose sight of the big picture. Now that you've completed your "Hello World", you can iteratively add more features and build more sophisticated agents. What's the first use case you're going to tackle?
